message:
Prepare for open-source: README, landing page, docs, and images

- Rewrite README for open-source audience with hero screenshot, feature table, self-hosting guide, and contributing section
- Update landing page: open-source positioning, GitHub links, self-host section, remove waitlist references
- Update Pi docs and changelog to use pi install one-liner
- Crop all feature images to 1.7:1 aspect ratio (top-aligned) and normalize to same dimensions

🔮 View transcript: https://agentlogs.ai/s/ip633t3ingy7hlpprzw2teyc

files.patch:
@@ -1,153 +1,164 @@ -# 🔮 AgentLogs +

+ +

-Capture and analyze coding agent session transcripts to understand AI assistant usage patterns. +

AgentLogs

-## What It Does +

+ Open-source observability for AI coding agents.
+ See what prompts work, learn from each other's workflows, and build institutional knowledge that compounds. +

-- **CLI**: Captures agent transcripts (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode) via hooks and uploads them -- **Web App**: Stores transcripts, analyzes patterns, displays insights +

+ Website · + Docs · + Discord · + Changelog +

-## Tech Stack +--- -TanStack Start + Cloudflare Workers + D1 (SQLite) + Drizzle ORM + BetterAuth (GitHub OAuth) + Tailwind CSS v4 +

+ +

-## Quick Start +AgentLogs captures and analyzes transcripts from AI coding agents (like Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Pi) to give your team visibility into how AI tools are used in their codebases. -### Prerequisites +**See it in action →** [Example transcript](https://agentlogs.ai/s/ijz0z090jxrmmfjsz9lkcq7j) -- [Bun](https://bun.sh/) v1.3.0+ -- GitHub OAuth App ([create one](https://github.com/settings/developers)) - - Homepage: `http://localhost:8787` - - Callback: `http://localhost:8787/api/auth/callback/github` +## Why AgentLogs? -### Setup +AI coding agents are becoming core to how teams write software. But right now, every session is a black box stored on the machine of the user. You can't see the context put into each session and there is no knowledge sharing between teammates. -```bash -# Install -bun install +AgentLogs fixes that: -# Configure -cp packages/web/.dev.vars.example packages/web/.dev.vars -# Edit .dev.vars with GitHub OAuth credentials +- **Team observability** — Dashboard with activity metrics, agent & model usage, and per-member breakdowns +- **Git integration** — Links sessions to the commits they produced. See which transcript wrote which code +- **Shared learning** — Browse and share your team's sessions to discover effective prompts and workflows -# Initialize database -bun db:migrate +| Team Dashboard | Git Integration | Session Browser | +| --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | +| ![Dashboard](https://agentlogs.ai/features/dashboard.png) | ![Git](https://agentlogs.ai/features/git.png) | ![List](https://agentlogs.ai/features/list.png) | -# Start -bun dev +## Supported Agents + +| Agent | Transcripts | Auto-sync | Commit Tracking | +| -------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- | --------- | --------------- | +| [Claude Code](https://agentlogs.ai/docs/agents/claude-code) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | +| [Codex](https://agentlogs.ai/docs/agents/codex) (experimental) | ✓ | ✓ | — | +| [OpenCode](https://agentlogs.ai/docs/agents/opencode) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | +| [Pi](https://agentlogs.ai/docs/agents/pi) | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | + +## Quick Start (using AgentLogs Cloud) + +### 1. Log in + +```bash +npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai ``` -Open http://localhost:8787 +### 2. Install the plugin for your agent -## Project Structure +**Claude Code** — inside Claude Code: ``` -packages/ -├── cli/ # Agent transcript capture tool -├── web/ # TanStack Start app on Cloudflare Workers -├── shared/ # TypeScript types and Zod schemas -├── opencode/ # OpenCode integration -└── e2e/ # End-to-end tests +/plugin marketplace add agentlogs/claude-code +/plugin install agentlogs ``` -## Commands +**Codex:** ```bash -# Development -bun dev # Start web app -bun agentlogs # Run CLI tool +codex mcp add agentlogs -- npx -y agentlogs mcp +``` -# Database -bun db:migrate # Run migrations -bun db:generate # Generate migrations -bun db:studio # Open Drizzle Studio -bun db:reset # Reset database +**OpenCode** — add to `opencode.json`: + +```json +{ "plugin": ["@agentlogs/opencode"] } +``` -# Quality -bun run check # Format check, lint, and type check -bun run lint # Lint code -bun run format # Format code +**Pi** — run inside Pi or from the terminal: -# Testing -bun run test:e2e # Run end-to-end tests +```bash +pi install npm:@agentlogs/pi ``` -## Local Plugin Development +### 3. Use your agent as usual -### Prerequisites +Transcripts are captured and uploaded automatically. View them at [agentlogs.ai](https://agentlogs.ai). -- Clone plugin repo: `git clone https://github.com/agentlogs/claude-code ../agentlogs-claude-code-plugin` -- Install plugin locally: `claude plugins install ../agentlogs-claude-code-plugin` +## CLI -### Setup +The CLI can also be used standalone for manual uploads: ```bash -# Switch to local development mode -bun run plugin:switch-dev -# Opens browser for authentication once -# Sets VI_CLI_PATH and VI_SERVER_URL in shell RC +# Interactive picker — browse transcripts from all agents +npx agentlogs upload -# Start local server -bun run dev +# Upload most recent transcript +npx agentlogs upload --latest -# Test with Claude -claude -p "test" -# Transcripts auto-upload to http://localhost:8787 -``` +# Sync all Claude Code transcripts +npx agentlogs claudecode sync -### Switch Modes - -```bash -bun run plugin:switch-prod # Use published npm package -bun run plugin:switch-dev # Use local CLI -bun run plugin:status # Check current mode +# Check auth status +npx agentlogs status ``` -### Debugging +See the full [CLI reference](https://agentlogs.ai/docs/cli/commands). -```bash -# View hook execution logs -tail -f logs/dev.log +## Self-Hosting -# Check authentication -bun agentlogs status +AgentLogs is fully open-source and can be self-hosted. -# Re-authenticate -bun agentlogs login agentlogs.ai -``` +### Prerequisites -## CLI Usage +- [Bun](https://bun.sh/) v1.3.0+ +- GitHub OAuth App ([create one](https://github.com/settings/developers)) + - Homepage: `http://localhost:8787` + - Callback: `http://localhost:8787/api/auth/callback/github` + +### Setup ```bash -bun agentlogs login agentlogs.ai # Authenticate -bun agentlogs status # Check login status -bun agentlogs claudecode upload transcript.jsonl # Upload transcript -bun agentlogs claudecode sync # Sync all local transcripts -bun agentlogs claudecode hook # Hook (receives via stdin) -bun agentlogs codex upload transcript.jsonl # Upload Codex transcript +git clone https://github.com/agentlogs/agentlogs.git +cd agentlogs +bun install + +# Configure environment +cp packages/web/.dev.vars.example packages/web/.dev.vars +# Edit .dev.vars with your GitHub OAuth credentials and a secret: +# GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=... +# GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=... +# BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=... (openssl rand -base64 32) +# BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:8787 +# WEB_URL=http://localhost:8787 + +# Initialize database +bun db:migrate + +# Start +bun dev ``` -## Environment Variables +Open http://localhost:8787 -Create `packages/web/.dev.vars`: +Point the CLI at your instance: ```bash -GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=your_client_id -GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=your_client_secret -BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=your_secret # openssl rand -base64 32 -BETTER_AUTH_URL=http://localhost:8787 -WEB_URL=http://localhost:8787 +npx agentlogs login localhost:8787 ``` -## Deployment +### Deploy to Cloudflare ```bash cd packages/web # Create D1 database wrangler d1 create agentlogs -# Run migrations +# Run remote migrations bun db:migrate:remote # Set secrets @@ -159,15 +170,62 @@ wrangler secret put BETTER_AUTH_SECRET bun run deploy ``` -## Database Schema +## Project Structure + +``` +packages/ +├── cli/ — CLI tool (npx agentlogs) +├── web/ — Web app (TanStack Start + Cloudflare Workers + D1) +├── shared/ — Shared types, schemas, transcript parsing, secret redaction +├── pi/ — Pi extension (@agentlogs/pi) +├── opencode/ — OpenCode plugin (@agentlogs/opencode) +└── e2e/ — End-to-end tests +docs/ — Documentation (Mintlify) +``` + +## Development + +```bash +# Start the web app +bun dev + +# Run CLI +bun agentlogs + +# Type check, lint, format +bun run check + +# Format code +bun run format + +# Run e2e tests +bun run test:e2e + +# Database commands +bun db:migrate # Run migrations +bun db:generate # Generate migrations from schema changes +bun db:studio # Open Drizzle Studio +bun db:reset # Reset local database +``` + +## Tech Stack + +- **Web**: [TanStack Start](https://tanstack.com/start) + [Cloudflare Workers](https://workers.cloudflare.com/) + [D1](https://developers.cloudflare.com/d1/) (SQLite) +- **ORM**: [Drizzle](https://orm.drizzle.team/) +- **Auth**: [BetterAuth](https://better-auth.com/) (GitHub OAuth + device flow) +- **Styling**: [Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/) + [shadcn/ui](https://ui.shadcn.com/) +- **CLI**: [Commander](https://github.com/tj/commander.js) +- **Quality**: [oxlint](https://oxc.rs/) + [oxfmt](https://oxc.rs/) + [tsgo](https://github.com/nicolo-ribaudo/tsgo) + +## Contributing -**Core Tables**: `repos`, `transcripts`, `analysis` -**Auth Tables**: `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification`, `device_code` +We welcome contributions! Please: -All data scoped by `userId` for multi-tenant isolation. +1. Fork the repo and create a branch +2. Make your changes +3. Run `bun run format` and `bun run check` +4. Open a PR -## Troubleshooting +## License -**Database issues**: `bun db:reset` -**Auth issues**: Verify callback URL and clear cookies -**Build errors**: `bun install && bun run check` +[MIT](LICENSE)

@@ -27,31 +27,13 @@ npx agentlogs login agentlogs.ai
 
 ### 2. Install the Extension
 
-```bash
-npm install -g @agentlogs/pi
-```
-
-### 3. Configure Pi
-
-Add the extension to your Pi settings. You can do this globally or per-project:
-
-**Global** (`~/.pi/agent/settings.json`):
+Run inside Pi or from the terminal:
 
-```json
-{
-  "extensions": ["@agentlogs/pi"]
-}
-```
-
-**Project-local** (`.pi/settings.json`):
-
-```json
-{
-  "extensions": ["@agentlogs/pi"]
-}
+```bash
+pi install npm:@agentlogs/pi
 ```
 
-The extension will be loaded automatically when you start Pi.
+This installs the package and registers the extension automatically. It will be loaded on next Pi startup.
 
 ## How It Works

@@ -27,16 +27,9 @@ Added support for [Pi](https://github.com/mariozechner/pi-coding-agent) transcri
 - **Branch-aware**: Properly handles Pi's conversation branching feature
 - **Interactive upload**: Pi sessions now appear in `npx agentlogs upload`
 
-Install the extension:
+Install the extension (run inside Pi or from the terminal):
 ```bash
-npm install -g @agentlogs/pi
-```
-
-Add to your Pi settings (`~/.pi/agent/settings.json`):
-```json
-{
-  "extensions": ["@agentlogs/pi"]
-}
+pi install npm:@agentlogs/pi
 ```
 
 See the [Pi integration guide](/agents/pi) for full setup instructions.
